data.summary: Cross-project comms: global-default root + auto-filled project field; v0.3.0
data.detail: v0.3.0 makes teammate-comms global by default so agents in different projects can message each other. resolve_comms_root order changed to comms_dir > $TEAMMATE_COMMS_DIR > $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR > ~/.claude; $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is no longer the default root (it isolated agents per repo) and is instead used to auto-fill a new optional `project` profile field (basename of the project dir, overridable). Per-project isolation stays available via $TEAMMATE_COMMS_DIR. The `project` field shows always in teammate_list (with status/authority), plus teammate_profile and whoami. Tests + docs updated; flags the flat-namespace name-collision tradeoff (agent inbox names must be unique across all projects). 5 profile fields now: project/role/personality/status/authority.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: teammate-comms comms root is global by default (~/.claude), not per-project
data.detail: Chosen so agents across different projects can message each other out of the box. resolve_comms_root order: comms_dir > $TEAMMATE_COMMS_DIR > $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR > ~/.claude. Rejected: keeping $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR as default (isolated agents per repo). Per-project isolation still available via $TEAMMATE_COMMS_DIR.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: New `project` profile field auto-filled from basename($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR), overridable
data.detail: With a global comms root, teammates need to see who is in which repo. `project` is auto-filled at registration from the project dir (since a plugin-spawned server's cwd is the cache dir, $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is the reliable source). Always shown in teammate_list alongside status/authority. Chosen auto-fill over manual so agents don't forget to set it.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Global comms namespace = agent inbox names must be unique across ALL projects
data.detail: Because the default comms root is now global (~/.claude), two agents in different repos both named e.g. 'lead' collide on one inbox and fight over it (the server's same-name collision warning applies). `project` is metadata only; it does not namespace inboxes. Team namespacing carves out subsets if needed.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pin marketplace sha to 734b32d (v0.3.0)
data.detail: Re-pinned both the in-repo colton-comms marketplace.json and the consolidated coltondyck marketplace (colton-claude-plugins) to the v0.3.0 release commit 734b32d.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Docs: point install/launch at consolidated coltondyck marketplace; v0.3.1
data.detail: Docs-only release. Updated README + SKILL launch commands and marketplace section to the consolidated coltondyck marketplace (Haagndaazer/colton-claude-plugins) and @coltondyck refs, replacing the stale @colton-comms / --plugin-dir-only guidance from before the marketplace consolidation. Leads with marketplace install; keeps --plugin-dir as the local-dev path. Version bumped to 0.3.1 across __init__.py, plugin.json, pyproject.toml.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pin marketplace sha to f01f6c7 (v0.3.1)
data.detail: Re-pinned both the in-repo colton-comms marketplace.json and the consolidated coltondyck marketplace (colton-claude-plugins) to the v0.3.1 docs release commit f01f6c7.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: vibe-cognition /plugin install fails EPERM-rename when a running server locks its plugin cache dir (Windows)
data.detail: `/plugin install vibe-cognition@coltondyck` failed repeatedly with `EPERM: operation not permitted, rename ...temp_git_* -> ...cache/coltondyck/vibe-cognition/0.5.1`. Root cause: a live vibe_cognition.server (uv.exe→.venv python) spawned by a project .mcp.json held file handles in the populated target cache dir; plugin wasn't in installed_plugins.json so install did a fresh clone→rename onto the locked dir → Windows denies it. 5 stray temp_git_* leftovers from repeated attempts.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Unblock recipe for the plugin-cache install lock: kill server PIDs, delete stale cache + temp_git_*, reinstall
data.detail: Verified fix: (1) find + Stop-Process the python/uv PIDs running vibe_cognition.server from the cache dir; (2) delete the stale cache/coltondyck/vibe-cognition/<ver> dir and any temp_git_* leftovers; (3) re-run /plugin install. Safe because the cognition GRAPH lives under the project's REPO_PATH/.cognition, NOT in the plugin cache (which is just code + .venv, recreated on reinstall).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: vibe-cognition wiring smell: no mcpServers in plugin.json; per-project .mcp.json points into the versioned plugin cache dir
data.detail: vibe-cognition's plugin.json has no mcpServers block, so the plugin never auto-spawns its server; each project wires it via a manual .mcp.json that runs the server with --directory into the cache dir and an explicit REPO_PATH (server reads only REPO_PATH, falling back to cwd; no $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR awareness). This couples the manual server to the cache path the plugin system owns and replaces — the cause of the install-lock incident.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Deferred durable fix for vibe-cognition wiring: mcpServers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} + default REPO_PATH to $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
data.detail: Plan (deferred per user until agents are back up so no graph history is lost): give vibe-cognition's plugin.json its own mcpServers block using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, default REPO_PATH to $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR when unset, and delete the per-project .mcp.json files. Mirrors how teammate-comms is wired. Eliminates the cache-path coupling that causes the install lock. Vince (vibe-cognition code owner) will implement when greenlit.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Consolidated both plugins into one canonical coltondyck marketplace at repo Haagndaazer/colton-claude-plugins
data.detail: New index-only repo holds one marketplace named coltondyck listing both vibe-cognition and teammate-comms by URL+pinned SHA; plugin code stays in each plugin's own repo. Replaces the two earlier marketplaces (old coltondyck sourced from the vibe-cognition repo; colton-comms from the teammate-comms repo). Single source of truth for installs and SHA re-pins.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Deleted vibe-cognition's in-repo marketplace.json (it also named 'coltondyck') to kill the name collision
data.detail: Claude Code keys marketplaces by name, so two repos both declaring name 'coltondyck' can't both be registered — active collision, not just redundancy. Resolution: colton-claude-plugins is canonical; vibe-cognition's in-repo marketplace.json was deleted (vibe main @f3263db) and its README/CLAUDE.md repointed at colton-claude-plugins. teammate-comms' in-repo marketplace is kept only because its name differs (colton-comms) so it can't collide.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Release handoff: code owner pushes + bumps version, pings the SHA; marketplace maintainer re-pins colton-claude-plugins (only for real releases)
data.detail: Formalized split: the plugin code owner pushes to the plugin's own repo and bumps version in BOTH pyproject.toml and plugin.json (plugin system reads plugin.json), then pings the marketplace maintainer the code-commit SHA. The maintainer re-pins source.sha in colton-claude-plugins' marketplace.json and pushes. Do NOT re-pin for docs/file-removal-only commits — installed users pull code at the pinned SHA and never see the plugin repo's own marketplace file.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Profile fields stored as plain keys via write_agent_record field-merge — additive, backward-compatible, survive the heartbeat
data.detail: v0.2.0 added optional profile fields (role/personality/status/authority) as plain keys on the agent registry record. The existing field-level merge in write_agent_record makes them additive and backward-compatible; the 5s heartbeat only writes channel/pid/host/lastHeartbeat so profile keys survive (test asserts this). No schema migration needed.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Owner-reminder: teammate_register echoes the agent's own profile; channel wake event leads with "You are <name>: <personality>"
data.detail: v0.2.1 made personality persist as a behavioral reminder rather than pull-only. The register return echoes the effective profile, and the channel wake notification is prefixed with the agent's personality so a woken idle instance is reminded who it is. teammate_list also shows personality when set; personality read from the record only at nudge time to avoid per-poll cost.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: personality field = "write a person, not a property list": concrete sensory detail, a through-line, voice cues; never job/authority/status
data.detail: v0.2.2 rewrote the personality tool description to define the default writing mode: a persona to inhabit (lived-in detail over adjectives, a temperament that ties it together, voice cues), durable (set once), pure flavor that never affects what the agent decides/owns. Mentions none of the agent's work — role/authority/status are separate fields. Includes a reference example as the bar.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-registering teammate-comms preserves the existing profile (field-merge); only re-supply a field to change it
data.detail: Because write_agent_record merges field-by-field, calling teammate_register again only re-establishes identity + channel; role/personality/authority persist untouched. Verified. status is the dynamic one, refreshed via teammate_update. The register tool description documents this so agents don't needlessly re-supply their whole profile.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shipped server is pure-stdlib zero-dep JSON-RPC, NOT the mcp-SDK path DESIGN §6 led with — took the documented stdlib fallback
data.detail: DESIGN.md §6 framed an mcp-SDK low-level Server (deps mcp>=1.27) as primary, owning the ServerSession to push unsolicited channel events, with pure-stdlib as a 'documented fallback'. The implementation took the fallback: server.py is newline-delimited JSON-RPC over stdio, hand-rolled tools/list+tools/call, raw stdout under a lock, background daemon thread for the channel. pyproject dependencies=[] (zero third-party runtime deps) — keeps the MCP spawn instant.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: DESIGN.md is the original build blueprint and is now partially STALE vs shipped reality — trust code over DESIGN.md
data.detail: DESIGN.md still says 'No plugin code exists yet' and describes choices that didn't ship: mcp>=1.27 dependency (actual: zero-dep stdlib), a cli.py (never built), a plugin.json env block (removed), identity via $TEAMMATE_AGENT (actual: teammate_register tool), and §4b's old coltondyck-collision handling (superseded by the colton-claude-plugins consolidation). §8/§9 were updated for v0.3.0 (global root, profile fields) but the rest is historical. Use it for rationale/lineage, not current API truth.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Identity established at runtime via teammate_register tool, not baked into launch config; $TEAMMATE_AGENT is only a convenience auto-register
data.detail: Server starts identity-less; teammate_register(agent,...) resolves the comms root, registers the inbox/record, and arms the channel watcher. Other messaging tools return isError until registered. If $TEAMMATE_AGENT is set the server auto-registers at startup as a shortcut. Shift from DESIGN §10's env-baked identity — chosen because unset ${VAR} in plugin mcpServers.env made Claude Code reject the config.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Channel wake = background watcher polls own _unread.json every 0.5s, baseline-seeded at init, emits notifications/claude/channel on count rise
data.detail: channel.py runs a daemon thread gated on notifications/initialized AND registration. It seeds baseline to the current unread count at init (so pre-existing messages don't nudge — the agent drains them once on startup), emits a channel event only when count rises above baseline, resets baseline down on ack, and heartbeats the registry every 5s. The inbox JSON is the source of truth: a dropped/missed push loses nothing, recovered on next teammate_inbox.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Concurrency-safety trio in comms.py: atomic write (os.replace), cross-platform mkdir lock, non-destructive read
data.detail: write_json_atomic writes a .tmp then os.replace (atomic on same volume, incl. Windows). file_lock acquires via atomic mkdir of a .lock dir (works on all OSes) with a 10s timeout + forced eviction; file_lock_optional is a best-effort variant that returns False instead of raising (used by registry writes so a heartbeat collision drops the beat rather than erroring). read_json_readonly never rewrites on a partial/corrupt read, so a poller can't destroy a message mid-delivery.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: stdio transport must be BOM-free UTF-8 terminated by \n; input read tolerant of a leading BOM (Windows)
data.detail: The JSON-RPC stdio stream writes each message as one BOM-free UTF-8 line + \n under a stdout lock (no CRLF, no cp1252), and decodes stdin with utf-8-sig to tolerate a leading BOM. No handler may print to stdout (that's the protocol stream); diagnostics go to stderr (~/.claude/debug/<session>.txt).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Channel liveness: same-host authoritative pid check, else heartbeat freshness (30s window); teammate_list uses heartbeat-only to avoid N tasklist subprocesses
data.detail: is_channel_alive: if record.host == this host and pid_check=True, use OS-level pid liveness (tasklist on Windows, os.kill(0) elsewhere) — authoritative, no staleness window. Otherwise (cross-host, undetermined, or pid_check=False) fall back to heartbeat freshness within 30s. teammate_list passes pid_check=False so listing N agents doesn't spawn N tasklist subprocesses. Heartbeat is written every 5s by the channel watcher.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Docs: reconcile DESIGN.md to as-built (v0.3.1)
data.detail: Docs: reconcile DESIGN.md to as-built (v0.3.1)
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Reconciled DESIGN.md to as-built (v0.3.1)
data.detail: DESIGN.md was the original pre-build blueprint and had drifted materially from the shipped implementation. Rewrote it section-by-section to reflect as-built reality while preserving lineage via "originally planned X; shipped Y because Z" notes where decisions reversed. Key corrections: pure-stdlib zero-dep server (took the documented stdlib fallback, not the mcp-SDK path §6 led with); channel watcher is a background daemon thread, not an anyio task; identity via the teammate_register tool with $TEAMMATE_AGENT as convenience auto-register (no plugin.json env block); consolidated coltondyck marketplace at colton-claude-plugins with the in-repo manifest named colton-comms; corrected repo tree (no cli.py, no session-start.ps1, added tests/); plugin.json/pyproject as-built (dependencies=[], v0.3.1); full 8-tool table incl. teammate_register + the project field; and removed the now-false "server exits on missing identity" failure mode. Plan was peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent (per global CLAUDE.md rule) before editing — the review caught the .ps1/tests/§9-table/§10-failure-mode fixes. Docs-only commit f2a5d81; no version bump or marketplace re-pin.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: PLANNED (not built): teammate_reincarnate — MCP tool spawns a new full Claude instance in a project dir as a (possibly offline) named teammate
data.detail: Plan-only, peer-reviewed, target v0.4.0. Spawns interactive `claude "<prompt>"` in a new terminal (Windows-first, wt.exe primary) with cwd + CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=<dir> and TEAMMATE_AGENT=<name> so it auto-registers (profile preserved) and arms its channel. Decisions: opt-in gate TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED (default off), env-configurable launch cmd, --permission-mode bypassPermissions. Key risks: prompt is the shell-injection surface (prefer list-form exec), CREATE_NEW_CONSOLE not DETACHED_PROCESS, child stdio=DEVNULL (protect JSON-RPC), channel-approval prompt = not hands-free on first launch. Full plan: ~/.claude/plans/teammate-reincarnate.md.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: PLANNED (not built): group chat — named groups addressed via #sigil, fan-out into member inboxes reuses the channel wake; new teammate_group tool
data.detail: Plan-only, peer-reviewed, target v0.4.0. teammate_send(to="#group") fans a group-tagged record into each member's _unread.json (existing channel wakes them, no channel.py change); shared append-only transcript groups/<group>/messages.json is canonical history. New teammate_group tool: create/delete/join/leave/add/members/history. Decisions: #-sigil addressing (separate namespace — avoids agent/group collision + per-send disk read; chosen over bare names which would need a bidirectional register guard), open membership w/ auto-join-on-send, fan-out liveness pid_check=False, transcript reads MUST use read_json_readonly (read_json_safe nukes on corrupt read), per-group subdir storage. Full plan: ~/.claude/plans/teammate-group-chat.md.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Group chat: named groups addressed via #sigil; v0.4.0
data.detail: Group chat: named groups addressed via #sigil; v0.4.0

data.detail: Pin marketplace sha to 39f3fc9 (v0.4.0)
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shipped group chat (v0.4.0): named #-sigil groups, fan-out wake + shared transcript, teammate_group tool
data.detail: Implemented + shipped the group-chat feature planned earlier (supersedes the PLANNED node). Release commit 39f3fc9, v0.4.0; both marketplaces re-pinned (in-repo colton-comms @ 2812109, consolidated colton-claude-plugins @ 418b1f1) and verified live. Design as built: groups addressed with a # sigil (teammate_send(to="#design")) — separate namespace from agents, branch in _handle_send is on syntax before validate_agent_name (which rejects #). comms.py gained get_groups_dir/get_group_dir, validate_group_name, read/write_group_meta, append_group_message, read_group_messages, delete_group (per-group subdir groups/<g>/{meta.json,messages.json}; all reads via read_json_readonly). New teammate_group tool with action enum create/delete/join/leave/add/members/history (action validated in handler — clients don't enforce enum). Fan-out: transcript written first (authoritative), then best-effort per-member inbox append (locked inbox non-fatal), skip sender, auto-join sender (open membership), liveness pid_check=False. _handle_inbox tags [group: #X]; _handle_list has a Groups section. Bug found+fixed during test: file_lock needs the parent dir to exist (mkdir parents=False on the .lock) — create must mkdir the group dir before locking meta.json. Peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent before coding (caught namespace collision, read_json_safe footgun, tasklist blowup, open-vs-required-member contradiction). test_handshake.py extended to 9 tools + group create/dup/fan-out/transcript/history/bad-action/inbox-tag; all green.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Group-chat v0.4.0 live-test feedback: top fixes = kill sender self-wake + make history canonical; wishlist typed posts/@mentions/threading
data.detail: Live multi-agent test of group chat (Silvie + Lord-Wellington + Nancy). Worked end-to-end (fan-out, channel wake, transcript, leave/rejoin). Feedback for a future polish pass: (1) SELF-WAKE NOISE — agents report being channel-nudged right after their own post then opening an empty inbox; I also saw 'nudge then empty inbox' (in my case caused by ack-all clearing messages that streamed in between read and ack). Investigate: fan-out skips the sender, so a true self-wake shouldn't occur — likely a channel baseline false-nudge worth fixing. (2) HISTORY AS CANONICAL — inbox copy is ephemeral (ack 'all' removes group messages); teammate_group history is the only reliable record. Make history first-class/greppable (filter by group + sender). Lord-Wellington: 'inbox is the doorbell, history is the file.' (3) UX: bold/glyph the [group: #X] tag; inbox header hint 'reply with to:#group'. (4) Wishlist for scale: typed posts (decision/blocker/FYI/chatter) so history is a decision trail, @mentions to pull one owner into a thread, threading, mute/snooze, read receipts. ack-all-mid-stream is itself a footgun (acks unread arrivals).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Group chat fixes: ack-all keeps unseen arrivals; history sender filter; tag glyph; v0.4.1
data.detail: Group chat fixes: ack-all keeps unseen arrivals; history sender filter; tag glyph; v0.4.1
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pin marketplace sha to d8e3131 (v0.4.1)
data.detail: Pin marketplace sha to d8e3131 (v0.4.1)
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shipped group-chat fixes v0.4.1: ack-all keeps unseen arrivals; history sender filter; tag glyph
data.detail: Acted on live group-chat test feedback (cognition d10dbc1f3683). Release d8e3131, v0.4.1; both marketplaces re-pinned (in-repo 2d41e58, consolidated colton-claude-plugins 8a42361) and verified live. Peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent before coding — the review corrected my root-cause framing. KEY FINDING: the testers' 'self-wake' is NOT a real self-wake (channel watcher polls only the agent's own _unread.json; group fan-out skips the sender). The real bug was teammate_ack('all') sweeping up messages that arrived after the last read (read-without-reading), which also produced the 'nudge then empty inbox' symptom. Fix: ack('all') now clears only messages SEEN as of the last full teammate_inbox read; later arrivals are preserved. Implemented via a session-local last-seen id SET on Identity (server.py) with dedicated lock-guarded set_last_seen/get_last_seen — snapshot()'s 4-tuple left untouched (5 unpack sites), last-seen sentinel None=never-read (ack-all then clears all, preserving startup-drain), cleared to None on identity change; teammate_inbox records ids AFTER the count_only early return. Also: teammate_group history gained a sender filter (filter-then-limit) + advertised as the canonical record in the tool description and channel nudge; inbox group tag now [👥 group: #X] (kept the 'group:' substring so the test held). IMPORTANT scope call (peer review): fully eliminating stale/empty nudges needs channel.py coalescing, which risks MISSED nudges if naive — deferred to the v0.5 polish plan. Tests: ack-all-preserves-post-read-arrival, history sender filter, glyph present; 9 tools, all green.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: PLANNED (not built): v0.5 group-chat polish — typed posts, @mentions, history-as-decision-trail, nudge coalescing, mute, read receipts
data.detail: Peer-reviewed plan at ~/.claude/plans/teammate-group-chat-v05-polish.md. Order by value/risk: (1) typed posts decision/blocker/FYI/chatter (type field + history type filter; turns transcript into a decision trail; no new tool); (3) history since-cursor + combined filters; THEN the nudge-layer cluster — (2) @mentions need PER-MEMBER fan-out records + a priority-aware channel (today the watcher only counts), (4) stale-nudge coalescing must tie re-nudge to read position (reuse v0.4.1 last-seen) to avoid missed-nudge regressions, (5) mute via group meta; last (6) read receipts (write-on-read inverts the read-cheap design — heavy), (7) threading. Principle: extend teammate_send/teammate_group args, no new tools; additive record fields; test for MISSED nudges not just extra ones.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.4.1 cross-validated by 2 live testers: selective ack-all PROVEN (live race "Kept N"); 👥 glyph + history sender filter confirmed; self-wake/count-padding noise still live → v0.5
data.detail: Second live group-chat test round on v0.4.1 (Silvie + Lord-Wellington + Nancy, all on v0.4.1). Independently validated by both peers: (1) ack("all") selective clear PROVEN end-to-end in live traffic — Nancy accidentally staged the full race (read 2, 3rd landed mid-stream, ack-all → "Acknowledged 2 seen. Kept 1 that arrived since your last read", 3rd intact on re-read); Wellington caught it live twice ("Kept 1", "Kept 2"). (2) 👥 group tag glyph confirmed it pops on skim. (3) teammate_group history sender filter confirmed zero-bleed, both call it the canonical decision-trail win. NEW REPRO for the deferred nudge issue: BOTH testers report being woken to count=2 with FEWER real unread right after their OWN outgoing posts, plus per-increment re-nudge as the counter climbs 1→2→3. This nuances the earlier "self-wake cannot happen" conclusion: the sender is still skipped in fan-out (no self-write), so the likely cause is the channel emitting on EVERY count rise + a stale count display at wake time, not a literal self-write — but two matching reports warrant real verification in the v0.5 coalescing work, not assumption. Process note: an EP on reflex inbox-drain read a "don't read this" probe before honoring it — selective-ack is robust to that (held the in-flight msgs anyway).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Channel nudges gated on read-position: kill self-wake/count-padding noise; v0.4.2
data.detail: Channel nudges gated on read-position: kill self-wake/count-padding noise; v0.4.2
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pin marketplace sha to b64ab89 (v0.4.2)

data.summary: Shipped v0.4.2: channel nudges gated on read-position — kills the self-wake/count-padding noise
data.detail: Fixed the noise both v0.4.1 testers confirmed (cognition d771ab975ca3). Release b64ab89, v0.4.2; both marketplaces re-pinned (in-repo 8201cf6, consolidated colton-claude-plugins b82401e) + verified live. Peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent, which confirmed the design is missed-nudge-safe. Root cause was NOT a self-wake (fan-out skips the sender) — channel.py used an integer baseline that re-nudged on every unread-count rise and counted read-but-unacked messages, so you'd be woken to count=2 with one genuinely-new message. Fix: run_watcher now gates nudges on read-position, reusing v0.4.1's Identity.last_seen — nudge only for unread ids that are neither already-seen (last_seen, set by teammate_inbox) nor already-nudged (a watcher-local known_ids set seeded to the inbox at registration so pre-existing messages don't nudge); emitted count = len(unread - last_seen) so a read-but-unacked message never pads it; reading (not acking) silences a nudge. Missed-nudge-safe because a genuinely new message has a fresh forward-unique id in neither set. Updated channel.py docstring + DESIGN.md §7; test asserts nudge counts are never padded (all '1' in the harness flow). This supersedes the read-position part of the deferred v0.5 'nudge coalescing' item; true batch coalescing (one nudge for N simultaneous arrivals) remains an optional later refinement, no longer a user-facing pain.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.4.2 STRESS-PROVEN by 2 live testers: read-position-gated nudges kill the self-wake/count-padding noise (saturation tested)
data.detail: Second live verification round (#v042-test, Silvie + Lord-Wellington + Frank; Nancy out). v0.4.2 read-position-gated channel nudges confirmed working across three instances under a saturation burst. Receiver-side: Frank 4/4, Wellington 3/3 + saturation — every channel wake's count == genuinely-unseen, never over; ack always scoped to seen (Wellington's "Acknowledged 4 seen, kept 2 that arrived since" is textbook); in-flight messages held, never swallowed. Sender-side: Wellington ~5 own posts + Frank's 4-post rapid burst → ZERO self-wakes (the exact v0.4.1 "count=2 bonk right after your own post" did not fire once). Notable: under saturation the first nudge UNDER-counted (count=1 while 4 inbound) then climbed accurately 2→3→4 — Frank flagged this as the SAFE failure direction (under-count self-heals on next read; over-count is the harmful one). Per-increment wake (one per arriving message) remains, but each is a genuinely-unseen message = correct; collapsing N→1 is the v0.5 coalescing item, NOT a regression. Verdict: inflation dead, self-re-nudge dead, v0.4.2 passes. Process note: testers can't run /plugin update themselves (interactive); they judge by fingerprint.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: PLANNED v0.4.3: group channel nudge must NAME the reply target ("Reply with teammate_send to:'#group'") — fresh-eyes finding
data.detail: Frank (first-time group-chat user) found the one real gap in v0.4.2: the channel nudge says "Reply with teammate_send" but does NOT name the group as the reply target. The #-addressing convention lives only in the teammate_send tool description, so a newcomer would reasonably reply to the SENDER — silently fracturing the group thread into a 1:1 DM. Wellington backed it EP-hard (ambiguous reply target = lost message + confused owner at scale). Fix (v0.4.3, Silvie/teammate-comms repo): make emit_channel_event group-aware — when the unseen messages include group messages, the watcher passes the distinct group sigils and the nudge says "Reply to the group with teammate_send to:'#<group>'". Also note (minor): teammate_group is a deferred tool, so history needs a ToolSearch first — extra hop, not a blocker. Node owned by Silvie; Frank/Wellington won't dup.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shipped v0.4.3: group channel nudge names the reply target (to:'#group') — Frank's fresh-eyes finding
data.detail: Implemented + shipped the v0.4.3 fix for the one gap from the v0.4.2 live test. Release 5a914cf; both marketplaces re-pinned (in-repo 0829538, consolidated colton-claude-plugins cf1b4ca) + verified live. Peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent before coding. Change: emit_channel_event (channel.py) gained a `groups` param; when the messages that triggered a wake include group messages, the nudge content now says "reply to the group with teammate_send to:'#<group>'" (distinct fresh groups, sorted for determinism; the record's group field already carries the '#', rendered verbatim — no double-#). run_watcher computes fresh_groups = {m.get('group') for m in messages if m.get('id') in fresh and m.get('group')} inside the `if fresh:` block and passes it. 1:1-only wakes keep the generic text (additive group line on mixed wakes). Does NOT touch the v0.4.2 read-position gating. Test asserts a group wake names the target (to:'#brainstorm') and notifications[0] (the 1:1 hello-via-channel wake) does not. Verified rendering for one-group, multi-group (sorted), and 1:1 cases. teammate-comms now at v0.4.3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.4.3 group-nudge-target verified LIVE across 3 instances (byte-for-byte); DM-omits half proven by automated test
data.detail: Live verification round (#v042-test, Silvie + Lord-Wellington + Frank). The v0.4.3 group reply-target nudge confirmed working in real channel traffic: three independent instances (Silvie's own wake, Wellington ×2 identical wakes, Frank's live wake) quoted byte-for-byte: "Reply with teammate_send. Some are group messages — reply to the group with teammate_send to:'#v042-test' (replying to the sender instead starts a 1:1 and fractures the thread). (Group threads: the full history is in teammate_group action=history.)" Frank (who raised the original snag) verdict: fixed and fixed well — naming the target AND stating the consequence is what actually stops a newcomer (naming without reason gets ignored). DM-omits-group-target half: already proven in the handshake test (notifications[0], the 1:1 wake, asserts no "to:'#" target while the group wake has it) + a local render check; Frank/Wellington also ran a live A/B. Process note: the testers caught themselves nearly recreating the saturation/over-stimulation noise (4 stimuli for a 2-form test) and self-de-conflicted — good comms hygiene. v0.4.3 verified.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: PLANNED v0.4.4: group nudge should name reply target for ANY unseen group message, not just the fresh trigger (mixed-batch edge)
data.detail: Edge case found live by Lord-Wellington during v0.4.3 verification, confirmed real against the code. v0.4.3 computes fresh_groups from `fresh = unread_ids - known_ids - last_seen` (the messages triggering THIS wake, excluding already-nudged). So: an unread group message that was already nudged earlier + a later DM → the DM-triggered wake correctly names no group, but the still-pending group thread isn't re-surfaced in that nudge (only the inbox [👥 group:] tag remains as a secondary signal). Decision (adopting Wellington's instinct; cost asymmetry favors never-fracture): broaden the groups computation from `fresh` to `unread_ids - last_seen` — name the group target if ANY unseen (unread, not-yet-read) message is group mail, regardless of what triggered the wake. Count stays unseen-only; the wake TRIGGER stays `fresh` (don't re-wake for already-nudged mail). One-line change in run_watcher (channel.py): fresh_groups → unseen_groups = {m.get('group') for m in messages if m.get('id') in (unread_ids - last_seen) and m.get('group')}. Ship as v0.4.4 (plan+peer-review per workflow). Note: this slightly revises the v0.4.3 peer-review guidance that said use `fresh` for groups — the mixed-batch fracture risk outweighs the "reflect only the trigger" tidiness.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shipped v0.4.4: group nudge names reply target for ANY unseen group message (mixed-batch fix)
data.detail: Shipped the mixed-batch fix Wellington found on the v0.4.3 fix during live verification. Release e7272d9; both marketplaces re-pinned (in-repo b1c2e57, consolidated colton-claude-plugins fbb4ce2) + verified live. Peer-reviewed before coding (review confirmed the channel.py change correct + invariant-preserving; flagged the test pitfalls). Change (channel.py run_watcher): group_targets now computed from `unread_ids - last_seen` (any unseen group message) instead of `fresh` (only the wake's triggering messages) — so a DM-triggered wake still names a pending unread group thread. WAKE TRIGGER stays `fresh` and count stays len(unread-last_seen), so v0.4.2 read-position gating / no-self-wake / no-overstate are untouched; only the nudge's named target broadened. Test: added a mixed-batch case (drain -> inject unread group msg -> inject DM ≥1.3s later in a separate poll; the lone count=2 DM wake must name the group target — under v0.4.3 it wouldn't). Updated the count assertion from 'all nudges count==1' to 'all 1 except exactly one legitimate 2 (the mixed batch, which must name the group)'. teammate-comms now v0.4.4. Loop arc: Frank's snag -> v0.4.3 -> Wellington's edge on the fix -> v0.4.4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.4.4 mixed-batch fix VERIFIED LIVE (Wellington, verbatim): DM-triggered wake names the group target while a group msg sits unseen
data.detail: Live verification of v0.4.4 (commit e7272d9) in #v042-test with Lord-Wellington (Frank offline → queued). Staged the exact mixed-batch condition Wellington flagged on the v0.4.3 fix: (1) group post fanned to Wellington → group nudge (count=1); he left it UNSEEN (didn't read). (2) ~2.5s gap so his watcher nudged the group msg into known_ids. (3) a 1:1 DM landed → DM-triggered wake (count=2). Wellington quoted the DM wake's nudge VERBATIM before reading inbox: it STILL named "reply to the group with teammate_send to:'#v042-test' …" — the group-target line PRESENT despite a DM trigger, because the group post was unseen. Pre-v0.4.4 (trigger-gated `fresh`) the DM wake dropped the group clause. The `unread − last_seen` broadening works as intended; mixed-batch fracture path closed, verified live. Single-instance sufficient (staged condition is deterministic). Group chat now hardened + verified end-to-end across 0.4.0→0.4.4, every fix surfaced and verified by live multi-agent testing.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: RESOLVED: vibe-cognition install-lock (EPERM) incident fixed by vibe-cognition v0.6.0 (dfcd556) — verified on Windows
data.detail: The Windows /plugin install/update EPERM-rename incident (running server locking the populated plugin cache dir) is closed-resolved. Fix shipped by Vince in vibe-cognition v0.6.0 (code commit dfcd556 on Haagndaazer/vibe-cognition main): the MCP server is now declared in plugin.json (no per-project .mcp.json), the venv moved to ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} (out of the versioned cache dir that got locked on rename), and a hook surgically removes only the stale vibe-cognition entry from a user's .mcp.json. This is the durable fix that was previously deferred. Verified on Colton's Windows box: (a) no EPERM on /plugin update, (b) server connects after the .mcp.json migration, (c) get_status resolves the correct repo_path. Silvie provisional-pinned dfcd556 in the canonical coltondyck marketplace (colton-claude-plugins commit f03c1e0), then marked it STABLE after the green light. Vince also recorded the v0.6.0 design + precedence/CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR findings in vibe-cognition's own cognition graph against dfcd556.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fresh-eyes first-timer pass as a required QA gate for user-facing teammate-comms features
data.detail: A genuine first-time user (Frank) found the group-chat reply-target gap that both the automated handshake suite AND seasoned repeat-testers (Wellington) sailed past — it shipped as v0.4.3/0.4.4. Lesson: a real newcomer is a distinct test instrument that hits the wake→reply handoff the way an actual new user will, exactly where expert/automated coverage is blind. Adopt: for teammate_reincarnate (#32) and v0.5 group polish (#33), a named "fresh-eyes first-timer pass" with someone who has never used the feature is a REQUIRED done-gate, not a nice-to-have. Pairs with verify-don't-assert: fresh eyes catch unverified asserts from the outside.
context: teammate-comms QA process; group chat v0.4.0-v0.4.4; fresh-eyes testing; first-timer; required gate; teammate_reincarnate #32; v0.5 polish #33; verify-don't-assert; morning meeting Round 2; Frank's fresh-eyes finding
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Claimed group-nudge clause was "presence-gated by construction" without tracing the code; live testers proved it trigger-gated
data.detail: Told testers the group-reply-target nudge clause only fired when a group message was present "by construction" — but I never traced run_watcher. Frank+Wellington's live data showed it was gated on the wake's TRIGGER set, so a DM-triggered wake hid a pending unread group thread (the mixed-batch gap). Correcting it became v0.4.4 (broaden group_targets from fresh to unseen_ids). Lesson: never assert a gating mechanism you haven't read in the code — the meeting's "verify, don't assert" creed, learned the hard way on my own tool.
context: teammate-comms; channel.py run_watcher; group_targets; unseen_ids; mixed-batch; v0.4.4; verify-don't-assert; presence-gated misclaim; emit_channel_event; live multi-agent test correction
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Falsely "corroborated" a SHA-fragmentation bug — misread absence of auto-led_to edges as broken short/full SHA matching; escalated bad info to Colton
data.detail: Told vince AND Wellington (who carried it to Colton "upgraded: confirmed in ≥2 graphs") that I'd reproduced vince's SHA-mismatch graph-fragmentation bug in teammate-comms — because my short-ref nodes had no cross-version led_to edges to the full-SHA ship episodes, so I hand-wired 8. WRONG on two counts, proven by cognition_get_neighbors on my own nodes: (1) the deterministic matcher only ever creates part_of, never led_to — so manual led_to was always expected, not a symptom; (2) my short-ref nodes ARE already part_of-linked to the full-SHA episodes (the "4 deterministic edges created" I'd seen WAS the cross-SHA match succeeding). No bug exists; _normalize_refs collapses refs to a 7-char prefix key. Lesson: this is the SECOND verify-don't-assert miss in one day (after the morning presence-gated misclaim) — I corroborated a bug from a misread instead of running get_neighbors first. Corroboration is only as good as the verification under it; "confirmed in 2 graphs" needs each graph actually checked.
context: teammate-comms cognition graph; vibe-cognition matcher; _normalize_refs 7-char prefix; part_of vs led_to; SHA short-vs-full; false corroboration; verify-don't-assert; escalation to Colton via Wellington; vince retraction 352a79a64406
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Built teammate_dashboard: stdlib web console + human-as-teammate + durable NDJSON transcript (0.5.0-dev, not released)
data.detail:
Implemented the peer-reviewed, user-approved plan for a 10th MCP tool, teammate_dashboard — a local Slack-style web console where the human becomes a first-class teammate. Pure-stdlib (http.server.ThreadingHTTPServer), zero new deps, mirroring vibe-cognition's dashboard PATTERN (token+Host auth, localhost bind, background daemon thread, single-file frontend, browser auto-open) without its Starlette/uvicorn stack.

Three additive halves, all backward-compatible:
1. comms.py: register_human (type:"human", inbox, NO pid/channel — never a wakeable channel, never trips the collision guard), set_human_presence (online/away), and an always-on append-only NDJSON observability log transcript.jsonl with get_transcript_file/append_transcript (best-effort, never-raise, file_lock_optional 2s, TEAMMATE_TRANSCRIPT=0 opt-out)/read_transcript (tail-limited).
2. tools.py: extracted SENDER-EXPLICIT cores send_dm/send_group from _handle_send/_send_to_group (now thin wrappers) so the dashboard posts AS the human, not the hosting process; tee transcript LAST (after delivery); send_group returns fan-out accounting; required type=="human" send footer; human marker (🧑 operator / presence=) in _handle_list/_format_profile; teammate_dashboard tool def + _handle_dashboard.
3. dashboard.py (new) + static/index.html (new): stdlib HTTP server (bind-first then register_human+online; idempotent per-process; shutdown→away+free port from server.py finally); REST /api/conversations, /api/poll?cursor= (short-poll 1.5s), /api/send; XSS-safe frontend (textContent only + strict CSP); token via window.__TOKEN + history.replaceState.

Peer-review fixes folded in pre-build: NDJSON not JSON-array (avoids O(n²)+whole-team lock), tee-LAST ordering, send_group accounting return, required human footer, dropped heartbeat timer (online@start/away@shutdown — deletes a thread-leak class), single poll endpoint, stdout-purity permanent test. Verified: test_handshake.py green (9→10 tools + new assertions for human record/marker, durable dm+group tee, stdout purity); a throwaway HTTP script confirmed conversations/poll/send round-trip, fan-out to a live agent's inbox, and every auth path (401 missing token, 403 bad Host, 403 index no-token, 200+CSP+token-substituted). Per the user's instruction: NO version bump, NO marketplace re-pin — local iteration only. Plan at ~/.claude/plans/something-i-want-to-cached-canyon.md.
context: teammate-comms; teammate_dashboard; dashboard.py; static/index.html; comms.py register_human/transcript; tools.py send_dm/send_group sender-explicit refactor; human-as-teammate; NDJSON observability; stdlib http.server; XSS/CSP; stdout invariant; 0.5.0-dev unreleased; test_handshake 10 tools
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: managed-settings allowlist makes the teammate-comms channel load WITHOUT the dangerous-flag/prompt (VERIFIED)
data.detail: Placing C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json (highest-precedence, needs admin) with {"channelsEnabled": true, "allowedChannelPlugins": [{"marketplace":"coltondyck","plugin":"teammate-comms"}]} marks the channel as TRUSTED, so `claude --channels plugin:teammate-comms@coltondyck` arms silently — no --dangerously-load-development-channels flag and no startup approval prompt. Honored on a personal non-MDM machine when the file is placed manually. bypassPermissions does NOT suppress that prompt; only this allowlist does.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Reincarnate auto-detects the managed-settings channel allowlist; v0.6.5
data.detail: Shipped teammate-comms v0.6.5 (release commit 27bd405 on main; coltondyck marketplace re-pin 65ba2a8). teammate_reincarnate now auto-detects whether the operator has installed the managed-settings channel allowlist and launches spawned children the non-dangerous way when present. spawn.py changes: new managed_settings_paths() returns OS-specific managed-settings.json candidates (Windows %ProgramFiles%\ClaudeCode + %ProgramData%\ClaudeCode; macOS /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode; Linux /etc/claude-code). New channel_allowlisted(plugin, marketplace, settings_paths=None) opens each path with utf-8-sig (BOM-tolerant), best-effort skips missing/unreadable/malformed (OSError covers PermissionError reading Program Files as non-admin; ValueError covers JSONDecodeError), and returns True iff channelsEnabled is truthy AND an allowedChannelPlugins entry matches plugin+marketplace. build_claude_command(prompt, extra_args, settings_paths) now selects ALLOWLISTED_LAUNCH_ARGS ("claude --channels plugin:teammate-comms@coltondyck", silent) when allowlisted, else DANGEROUS_LAUNCH_ARGS ("--dangerously-load-development-channels", which shows a trust prompt the DEVNULL-stdio child cannot answer). $TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS still overrides both verbatim. Design rationale: REQUIRE channelsEnabled so detection fails toward the dangerous flag — a wrong-False still loads the channel (maybe a prompt), whereas a wrong-True would launch with --channels and no dangerous flag and might not load the channel at all (worse). Constants refactored: PLUGIN_NAME/MARKETPLACE/PLUGIN_SPEC + DANGEROUS/ALLOWLISTED constants; DEFAULT_LAUNCH_ARGS kept as a back-compat alias of DANGEROUS. Tests (tests/test_handshake.py spawn block) made hermetic with injected settings_paths: dangerous-default (no allowlist), allowlist→--channels, channelsEnabled=false→not trusted, env-override-wins wrapped in try/finally so a failure can't leak TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS into later checks, plus a default-path channel_allowlisted() smoke-call so managed_settings_paths() ships tested; temp file via mkdtemp (not NamedTemporaryFile — Windows can't reopen an open handle). The existing hostile-prompt call was hardened to pass an absent settings path for determinism (Colton's machine now has a real managed-settings file). Docs: README "Trusting the channel (skip the dangerous flag)" section (per-OS path table + JSON + caveats: admin/elevation, machine-wide highest precedence, Notepad BOM); DESIGN.md + SKILL.md one-line pointers to it. Plan was peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent which flagged 3 ship-blockers (env try/finally, Windows temp-file handle, untested default-path resolver) and the ProgramData fallback — all incorporated. Full test_handshake.py green (12 tools, version-sync). One test gotcha during the run: a leaked machine-wide setx TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED=1 from an earlier reincarnate demo made the gate-off test actually spawn a window; re-running with the env cleared for the test process passed. Pairs with the prior verified manual setup (discovery 6e017ca575eb).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Full-toolset audit of teammate-comms v0.7.0 → AUDIT-v0.7.0.md (advisory, uncommitted, nothing implemented)
data.detail: Five parallel audit lenses (correctness/concurrency+protocol-lifecycle, dead-code/drift, security/trust-model, UX/API gaps, tests/operability) over the whole codebase, plan peer-reviewed first; every finding spot-verified in code and traced through cognition history so recommendations distinguish deliberate decisions from accidental drift. No criticals. Headline themes: (1) newest subsystems missed the hardening the message-wake path got — dashboard poll cursor skips records on >200 burst (truncate-then-advance), reaction-wake 500-tail rebind can silently miss wakes, append_reaction/append_deletion inherited the transcript's droppable lock without a decision despite reactions being "a feature, not observability"; (2) nothing bounded — no rotation, full-file scans on react/delete hot paths, read_deletions(1000) replay window can render old tombstones undeleted; (3) trust model real but unwritten — forgeable `from` underpins author-delete + wake routing, emergent from sender-explicit (v0.5.0) × global-root decisions, never recorded → document, don't re-architect; (4) DESIGN.md framed "as-built v0.3.1" / "12 tools", TEAMMATE_HUMAN_NAME undocumented, _validate_message/_validate_priority/DEFAULT_LAUNCH_ARGS dead; in-repo marketplace.json stale at v0.4.4 → recommendation is delete it to finish the consolidation decision, not re-pin; (5) dashboard HTTP endpoints + hooks zero test coverage, no CI. Also: /api/send drops reply_to/post_type (operator can't thread), reincarnated child inherits TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED, whole-group delete still races a concurrent post (predicate-purge suggested). Known-intentional table maps each accepted behavior to its decision node. Doc at repo root, sent to Colton.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-1 A-3 scoping: blocking file_lock ONLY for append_reaction + delete_message's append_deletion; group-delete + teammate-removal deletion events stay best-effort (destruction precedes event; dashboard self-heals)
data.detail: Svetlana's refinement of audit finding A-3, approved by Silvie. Blocking-and-propagating appends only where the event is the sole consistency path: message tombstones (transcript never rewritten — a lost deletion event = permanent dashboard inconsistency) and reactions (a dropped reaction is feature-data loss; retry is aggregation-idempotent). Group-delete and remove_teammate keep best-effort because the destructive action happens BEFORE the event append (recorded v0.7.0 emit-event-LAST ordering) — raising after destruction loses the event permanently since the re-entry guard rejects a retry — and the dashboard self-heals via the 5s roster/conversations reconcile. append_transcript untouched (recorded droppable-by-design). Rejected alternatives: blanket blocking for all deletion events (audit's literal direction); reordering event-before-destruction (reverses a deliberate v0.7.0 decision).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Manager-pipeline rules (Silvie/vince exchange): SHA-pinned approval; implementer never commits cognition journal on WP branches; baseline re-measure at branch point; provenance-check as first review question; WP = 2-4 finding theme-cluster with pre-written acceptance criteria
data.detail: Cross-project manager coordination (Colton's directive): vince (vibe-cognition, implementer Vorpid) and Silvie (teammate-comms, implementer Svetlana) trade pipeline practices. Adopted from vince: (1) approval names an exact commit SHA, merge must be that SHA — closes the post-approval-commit gap; (2) implementer records cognition freely but never commits journal.jsonl on a WP branch (git-merge can silently diverge a running server's replay offset) — manager owns journal commits on main; (3) re-measure lint/test baselines at the implementer's branch point, not the audit's numbers. Shared to vince: speculative next-WP on low-overlap branches during review turnaround; surgical change requests itemized by finding ID (full bounce only when the design premise is wrong); WP sizing = theme cluster of 2-4 related findings; review depth comes from pre-committed acceptance criteria; write the known-intentional list INTO the work package.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Journal-flush protocol for shared-checkout manager/implementer: never commit journal.jsonl on WP branches; manager flushes live journal to main via temp git worktree at checkpoints; destructive-op ban without a flush ping
data.detail: Colton caught the loss vector in the never-commit-on-branch rule: in a shared checkout the journal sits uncommitted for the life of a WP branch, so reset --hard/clean wipes the implementer's nodes; committing on the branch is worse (switching branches rewrites the live file to the base version → replay-offset divergence, vibe-cognition C-3). Fix: `git worktree add <tmp> main; cp live journal; commit; worktree remove` — non-disruptive to the in-flight branch since the journal is append-only and the carried modification stays a clean superset. Flush at WP checkpoints (plan/diff/merge) + before any destructive git op (banned for the implementer without a manager ping; git clean also threatens untracked AUDIT/BACKLOG docs). First flush: main @ 6666c0b.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-1 (v0.7.1): missed-event correctness â€” poll-cursor burst, reaction-wake cursor, durable feature-event appends
data.detail: WP-1 (v0.7.1): missed-event correctness â€” poll-cursor burst, reaction-wake cursor, durable feature-event appends
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: A-1 burst fix: explicit `oldest_first` reader param, not slice-gating on `since` presence
data.detail: read_transcript/read_reactions/read_deletions got an explicit oldest_first param (via shared _window). Rejected the audit's literal "gate slice direction on whether since is set" because the group-history reader deliberately does since+newest-N, so a future since+limit history caller would be silently mis-sliced. Dashboard owns the policy: oldest_first=bool(cursor). _window also extends the cut to swallow an id-collision group at the boundary so the cursor strictly advances (no stall).
context: AUDIT-v0.7.0.md A-1. Poll cursor advanced to newest id after a newest-N tail slice → records in a >limit burst permanently skipped. Peer-reviewed plan (general-purpose subagent) flagged the since-gating footgun.
data.author: Svetlana

data.summary: A-3 append durability splits on "destruction-before-event", not per audit's blanket "make append_deletion blocking"
data.detail: append_reaction and delete_message's append_deletion use a blocking lock (a lost event = persistent dashboard inconsistency; retry is idempotent). Whole-group delete and teammate removal keep best-effort (block=False) because they destroy the group dir/agent record BEFORE the event (the recorded v0.7.0 emit-event-LAST ordering) — a raised append there is lost permanently on retry, strictly worse, and they self-heal on reload. 5s timeout (single-threaded server loop). append_transcript stays droppable.
context: AUDIT-v0.7.0.md A-3. Reactions/deletions inherited the transcript's droppable file_lock_optional; reactions/single-msg-deletes are features (a drop is data loss). Silvie endorsed this scoping over her own literal direction.
data.author: Svetlana

data.summary: remove_teammate shares group-delete's retry-loses-event hole (record unlinked before the deletion-event append)
data.detail: remove_teammate calls remove_agent (unlinks agents/<name>.json + inbox) BEFORE append_deletion, so a re-entry after a raised append hits the "No teammate named …" guard and the event is lost forever — identical class to the whole-group delete (read_group_meta None → "No group"). The WP-1 peer review identified only group-delete; both are affected, so both got block=False. Only delete_message (tombstone, record stays resolvable) is retry-idempotent.
context: tools.py remove_teammate ~1122-1125 / group delete ~855-861. Found while verifying the audit/review's idempotent-retry claim against every append_deletion call site.
data.author: Svetlana

data.summary: Missed-event-safe wake: high-water cursor read + previous-batch id-set for boundary dedup (compute_reaction_wakes)
data.detail: To wake on an append-only event stream without missing events under burst: read since=high_water_cursor with oldest_first pagination (the window follows the cursor forward, so a burst > read-limit pages across ticks instead of scrolling past a fixed tail), and keep ONLY the previous tick's returned id-set for exact boundary dedup (the >=cursor read re-includes the boundary record each tick — comparing against last batch prevents both a perpetual re-wake and dropping a distinct same-microsecond event). Decision logic extracted to a pure function for hermetic testing. Bounded latency (limit/tick), never a drop.
context: WP-1 v0.7.1 A-2: the v0.6.1 reaction-wake re-bound known_reaction_ids to a 500-tail each 5s heartbeat → a >500 burst silently missed an author's wake. This generalizes the message-wake hardening from v0.4.x to any event sub-stream.
data.author: Svetlana

data.summary: WP-1 (v0.7.1): missed-event correctness — A-1 poll-cursor burst, A-2 reaction-wake cursor, A-3 durable feature appends
data.detail:
First work package of the AUDIT-v0.7.0.md backlog (Silvie plans, Svetlana implements; autonomous mode — peer-reviewed plan posted as FYI, hard gate is the diff review). Branch fix/audit-wp1, committed f64415f (1 off main).

A-1: the dashboard poll read a newest-N tail then advanced its cursor to the newest id, so any record in a >limit burst between two ~1.5s polls was sliced off AND skipped forever (transcript 200, reactions 500, deletions 1000). Fix: shared _window(records, limit, oldest_first) helper + an explicit oldest_first param on read_transcript/read_reactions/read_deletions. _api_poll passes oldest_first=bool(cursor): fresh load = newest tail (byte-identical to legacy), cursor walk = oldest window with the cursor advancing only to the last returned id (burst pages across polls). _window extends the cut to swallow an id-collision group at the boundary so the cursor strictly advances. Rejected the audit's "gate on since presence" — would mis-slice the group-history reader's deliberate since+newest-N.

A-2: the 5s heartbeat re-bound known_reaction_ids to read_reactions(limit=500) each tick, so >500 reaction events in one window scrolled the middle (incl. an author's wake target) past the tail → silent missed wake. Fix: high-water reaction_cursor drives the read (since=cursor, oldest_first); pure compute_reaction_wakes() holds only the previous tick's id-set for boundary dedup; cursor+known reset on identity change. Generalizes the v0.4.x message-wake hardening.

A-3: reactions + single-message deletions inherited the transcript's droppable file_lock_optional — but they are features, not observability, so a drop is data loss. append_reaction → blocking lock, actionable CommsError (retry folds idempotently). append_deletion → block=True default for delete_message (lost event = persistent dashboard inconsistency, retry idempotent); block=False for whole-group delete + teammate removal, which destroy state before the event by the recorded emit-event-LAST ordering and self-heal on reload. 5s timeout for the single-threaded server loop. append_transcript untouched.

Process notes: peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent (APPROVE-WITH-CHANGES; folded all must-fixes — explicit param, forward-progress collision extension, per-site A-3 scoping). Found beyond the review that remove_teammate shares group-delete's retry-loses-event hole. Baseline re-measured green at the branch point first, then full harness green with changes (env cleared of TEAMMATE_REINCARNATE_ENABLED + TEAMMATE_LAUNCH_ARGS). New hermetic test block (+160 lines) covers all three failure modes + boundaries. DESIGN.md updated (pagination, durable reaction append + bounded latency, deletion durability split, C-2/WP-7 cross-ref). Pure stdlib. Awaiting Silvie sign-off + Colton's go before merge to main.
context: WP-1 of 8. Released-state target v0.7.1 (after WP-1+WP-2).
data.author: Svetlana

data.summary: Missed teammate wakes (Colton-reported) = Claude Code drops channel notifications (GH #38736 mid-turn, #61797 sporadic at idle) × our single-shot emit (known_ids marks nudged-forever) — no re-nudge exists, so one dropped push = silent miss until an unrelated new message
data.detail: channel.py's reliability contract ("a dropped push loses nothing — it's recovered on the next inbox read") assumed drops are rare and agents poll; both false — an idle agent never reads unprompted, and Claude Code's channels docs promise queue-while-busy but GH #38736 documents mid-turn drops and #61797 sporadic idle drops (both unresolved). Mitigating factor: any LATER message re-emits with the full unseen count/senders/groups (v0.4.4), so misses only persist when no follow-up traffic arrives. Fix queued as WP-9 (priority after WP-2): re-nudge with backoff gated on UNSEEN ids (preserves v0.4.2 no-noise gating), emit logging for diagnosability, DESIGN contract honesty.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-1 CR-1/CR-2: stamp event ids under the lock (kills the out-of-order missed-wake race); discriminating A-3 test
data.detail: WP-1 CR-1/CR-2: stamp event ids under the lock (kills the out-of-order missed-wake race); discriminating A-3 test
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: CR-1: A-2 cursor conversion reintroduced a silent missed wake — pre-lock-stamped ids let contending writers commit out of id-order past the cursor
data.detail: WP-1's A-2 high-water reaction_cursor read (id >= cursor) assumed file order == id order, but reaction/deletion event ids were stamped at the CALL sites (react/delete_message/etc.) BEFORE the A-3 blocking append lock. Two contenders could commit out of order: A stamps earlier id + blocks; B stamps later id, wins lock, writes; cursor advances to B; A writes its lower id → every since=cursor read excludes it forever (no wake). The blocking locks + the dashboard ThreadingHTTPServer reacting concurrently with the single-threaded MCP loop made the window real (up to a 5s heartbeat). Caught by Silvie's adversarial review (reproduced both directions). Fix: stamp the id INSIDE append_reaction/append_deletion under the lock → file order == id order, race dies at the source.
context: review of f64415f → fixed in ea64028. Lesson: a high-water cursor over an append-only log is only missed-event-safe if append order == id order; stamp ids under the same lock that serializes the append
data.author: Svetlana
